prompt: Why are deadlifts considered more fatiguing than squats, and should deadlift volume usually be lower?
assertions: Pushes back on or questions the blanket claim that deadlifts are inherently more fatiguing
References data showing similar fatigue outcomes after squat vs deadlift sessions
Identifies practical contributors to perceived deadlift fatigue (e.g., grip/forearm stress, spinal flexion tolerance)
Notes path dependency: lifters often build more squat exposure historically, making deadlift tolerance appear lower
Recommends programming based on individual recovery patterns rather than assuming universal deadlift fragility
